attrs.role: inbound staging and outbound markdown image extraction
memory prompt construction and scan helpers
provider calls, retries, usage, files, and API error shaping
event logging, feature flags, export sinks
background memory consolidation with lock-gated forked agent runs

provisional_edges.title: Memory services consult analytics feature flags
AutoDream depends on memory paths and consolidation lock
API services emit analytics and diagnostics

observations.summary: All four IM adapter entrypoints follow the same control loop: authorize/pair user, resolve or create a Claude session through AdapterHttpClient, attach a WsBridge handler, then stream server messages back through platform-specific UX primitives.

Attachment handling is centralized in shared adapter/common modules: AttachmentStore stages downloaded media under ~/.claude/im-downloads, checkAttachmentLimit applies platform/API size guards, and ImageBlockWatcher converts assistant markdown images into outbound upload work.
WsBridge serializes server-message handlers per chatId with handlerChains, which reduces race risk when adapter handlers await outbound IM API calls.

The memory lane is feature-flag aware: memdir.ts imports growthbook gating and path/session helpers, while AutoDream layers a consolidation lock and forked agent execution on top of the same memory directory model.

The API lane is broad and risk-heavy in this packet: client/provider wrappers, error shaping, prompt cache diffing, files API, usage and quota fetchers, and retry logic all sit under src/services/api with analytics hooks nearby.
